This often\\n26: occurs due to changes in the modelling logic (bug fixes or enhancements), or in\\n27: random sampling procedures.\\n28: \\n29: - |Fix| ``inertia_`` attribute of :class:`cluster.KMeans` and\\n30:   :class:`cluster.MiniBatchKMeans`.\\n31: \\n32: Details are listed in the changelog below.\\n33: \\n34: (While we are trying to better inform users by providing this information, we\\n35: cannot assure that this list is complete.)\\n36: \\n37: Changelog\\n38: ---------\\n39: \\n40: :mod:`sklearn.cluster`\\n41: ......................\\n42: \\n43: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.KMeans` where rounding errors could\\n44:   prevent convergence to be declared when `tol=0`. :pr:`17959` by\\n45:   :user:`Jérémie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n46: \\n47: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.KMeans` and\\n48:   :class:`cluster.MiniBatchKMeans` where the reported inertia was incorrectly\\n49:   weighted by the sample weights. :pr:`17848` by\\n50:   :user:`Jérémie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n51: \\n52: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.MeanShift` with `bin_seeding=True`. When\\n53:   the estimated bandwidth is 0, the behavior is equivalent to\\n54:   `bin_seeding=False`.\\n55:   :pr:`17742` by :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n56: \\n57: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.AffinityPropagation`, that\\n58:   gives incorrect clusters when the array dtype is float32.\\n59:   :pr:`17995` by :user:`Thomaz Santana  <Wikilicious>` and\\n60:   :user:`Amanda Dsouza <amy12xx>`.\\n61: \\n62: :mod:`sklearn.decomposition`\\n63: ............................\\n64: \\n65: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in\\n66:   :func:`decomposition.MiniBatchDictionaryLearning.partial_fit` which should\\n67:   update the dictionary by iterating only once over a mini-batch.\\n68:   :pr:`17433` by :user:`Chiara Marmo <cmarmo>`.\\n69: \\n70: - |Fix| Avoid overflows on Windows in\\n71:   :func:`decomposition.IncrementalPCA.partial_fit` for large ``batch_size`` and\\n72:   ``n_samples`` values.\\n73:   :pr:`17985` by :user:`Alan Butler <aldee153>` and\\n74:   :user:`Amanda Dsouza <amy12xx>`.\\n75: \\n76: :mod:`sklearn.ensemble`\\n77: .......................\\n78: \\n79: - |Fix| Fixed bug in `ensemble.MultinomialDeviance` where the\\n80:   average of logloss was incorrectly calculated as sum of logloss.\\n81:   :pr:`17694` by :user:`Markus Rempfler <rempfler>` and\\n82:   :user:`Tsutomu Kusanagi <t-kusanagi2>`.\\n83: \\n84: - |Fix| Fixes :class:`ensemble.StackingClassifier` and\\n85:   :class:`ensemble.StackingRegressor` compatibility with estimators that\\n86:   do not define `n_features_in_`. :pr:`17357` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n87: \\n88: :mod:`sklearn.feature_extraction`\\n89: .................................\\n90: \\n91: - |Fix| Fixes bug in :class:`feature_extraction.text.CountVectorizer` where\\n92:   sample order invariance was broken when `max_features` was set and features\\n93:   had the same count. :pr:`18016` by `Thomas Fan`_, `Roman Yurchak`_, and\\n94:   `Joel Nothman`_.\\n95: \\n96: :mod:`sklearn.linear_model`\\n97: ...........................\\n98: \\n99: - |Fix| :func:`linear_model.lars_path` does not overwrite `X` when\\n100:   `X_copy=True` and `Gram='auto'`. :pr:`17914` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n101: \\n102: :mod:`sklearn.manifold`\\n103: .......................\\n104: \\n105: - |Fix| Fixed a bug where :func:`metrics.pairwise_distances` would raise an\\n106:   error if ``metric='seuclidean'`` and ``X`` is not type ``np.float64``.\\n107:   :pr:`15730` by :user:`Forrest Koch <ForrestCKoch>`.\\n108: \\n109: :mod:`sklearn.metrics`\\n110: ......................\\n111: \\n112: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :func:`metrics.mean_squared_error` where the\\n113:   average of multiple RMSE values was incorrectly calculated as the root of the\\n114:   average of multiple MSE values.\\n115:   :pr:`17309` by :user:`Swier Heeres <swierh>`.\\n116: \\n117: :mod:`sklearn.pipeline`\\n118: .......................\\n119: \\n120: - |Fix| :class:`pipeline.FeatureUnion` raises a deprecation warning when\\n121:   `None` is included in `transformer_list`. :pr:`17360` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n122: \\n123: :mod:`sklearn.utils`\\n124: ....................\\n125: \\n126: - |Fix| Fix :func:`utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` so that all test\\n127:   cases support the `binary_only` estimator tag.\\n128:   :pr:`17812` by :user:`Bruno Charron <brcharron>`.\\n129: \\n130: .. _changes_0_23_1:\\n131: \\n132: Version 0.23.1\\n133: ==============\\n134: \\n135: **May 18 2020**\\n136: \\n137: Changelog\\n138: ---------\\n139: \\n140: :mod:`sklearn.cluster`\\n141: ......................\\n142: \\n143: - |Efficiency| :class:`cluster.KMeans` efficiency has been improved for very\\n144:   small datasets. In particular it cannot spawn idle threads any more.\\n145:   :pr:`17210` and :pr:`17235` by :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n146: \\n147: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.KMeans` where the sample weights\\n148:   provided by the user were modified in place. :pr:`17204` by\\n149:   :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n150: \\n151: \\n152: Miscellaneous\\n153: .............\\n154: \\n155: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in the `repr` of third-party estimators that use a\\n156:   `**kwargs` parameter in their constructor, when `changed_only` is True\\n157:   which is now the default. :pr:`17205` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n158: \\n159: .. _changes_0_23:\\n160: \\n161: Version 0.23.0\\n162: ==============\\n163: \\n164: **May 12 2020**\\n165: \\n166: \\n167: Enforcing keyword-only arguments\\n168: --------------------------------\\n169: \\n170: In an effort to promote clear and non-ambiguous use of the library, most\\n171: constructor and function parameters are now expected to be passed as keyword\\n172: arguments (i.e. using the `param=value` syntax) instead of positional. To\\n173: ease the transition, a `FutureWarning` is raised if a keyword-only parameter\\n174: is used as positional. In version 1.0 (renaming of 0.25), these parameters\\n175: will be strictly keyword-only, and a `TypeError` will be raised.\\n176: :issue:`15005` by `Joel Nothman`_, `Adrin Jalali`_, `Thomas Fan`_, and\\n177: `Nicolas Hug`_. See `SLEP009\\n178: <https://scikit-learn-enhancement-proposals.readthedocs.io/en/latest/slep009/proposal.html>`_\\n179: for more details.\\n180: \\n181: Changed models\\n182: --------------\\n183: \\n184: The following estimators and functions, when fit with the same data and\\n185: parameters, may produce different models from the previous version. This often\\n186: occurs due to changes in the modelling logic (bug fixes or enhancements), or in\\n187: random sampling procedures.\\n188: \\n189: - |Fix| :class:`ensemble.BaggingClassifier`, :class:`ensemble.BaggingRegressor`,\\n190:   and :class:`ensemble.IsolationForest`.\\n191: - |Fix| :class:`cluster.KMeans` with ``algorithm=\\\"elkan\\\"`` and\\n192:   ``algorithm=\\\"full\\\"``.\\n193: - |Fix| :class:`cluster.Birch`\\n194: - |Fix| `compose.ColumnTransformer.get_feature_names`\\n195: - |Fix| :func:`compose.ColumnTransformer.fit`\\n196: - |Fix| :func:`datasets.make_multilabel_classification`\\n197: - |Fix| :class:`decomposition.PCA` with `n_components='mle'`\\n198: - |Enhancement| :class:`decomposition.NMF` and\\n199:   :func:`decomposition.non_negative_factorization` with float32 dtype input.\\n200: - |Fix| :func:`decomposition.KernelPCA.inverse_transform`\\n201: - |API| :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n202:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor`\\n203: - |Fix| ``estimator_samples_`` in :class:`ensemble.BaggingClassifier`,\\n204:   :class:`ensemble.BaggingRegressor` and :class:`ensemble.IsolationForest`\\n205: - |Fix| :class:`ensemble.StackingClassifier` and\\n206:   :class:`ensemble.StackingRegressor` with `sample_weight`\\n207: - |Fix| :class:`gaussian_process.GaussianProcessRegressor`\\n208: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.RANSACRegressor` with ``sample_weight``.\\n209: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.RidgeClassifierCV`\\n210: - |Fix| :func:`metrics.mean_squared_error` with `squared` and\\n211:   `multioutput='raw_values'`.\\n212: - |Fix| :func:`metrics.mutual_info_score` with negative scores.\\n213: - |Fix| :func:`metrics.confusion_matrix` with zero length `y_true` and `y_pred`\\n214: - |Fix| :class:`neural_network.MLPClassifier`\\n215: - |Fix| :class:`preprocessing.StandardScaler` with `partial_fit` and sparse\\n216:   input.\\n217: - |Fix| :class:`preprocessing.Normalizer` with norm='max'\\n218: - |Fix| Any model using the `svm.libsvm` or the `svm.liblinear` solver,\\n219:   including :class:`svm.LinearSVC`, :class:`svm.LinearSVR`,\\n220:   :class:`svm.NuSVC`, :class:`svm.NuSVR`, :class:`svm.OneClassSVM`,\\n221:   :class:`svm.SVC`, :class:`svm.SVR`, :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegression`.\\n222: - |Fix| :class:`tree.DecisionTreeClassifier`, :class:`tree.ExtraTreeClassifier` and\\n223:   :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier` as well as ``predict`` method of\\n224:   :class:`tree.DecisionTreeRegressor`, :class:`tree.ExtraTreeRegressor`, and\\n225:   :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingRegressor` and read-only float32 input in\\n226:   ``predict``, ``decision_path`` and ``predict_proba``.\\n227: \\n228: Details are listed in the changelog below.\\n229: \\n230: (While we are trying to better inform users by providing this information, we\\n231: cannot assure that this list is complete.)\\n232: \\n233: Changelog\\n234: ---------\\n235: \\n236: ..\\n237:     Entries should be grouped by module (in alphabetic order) and prefixed with\\n238:     one of the labels: |MajorFeature|, |Feature|, |Efficiency|, |Enhancement|,\\n239:     |Fix| or |API| (see whats_new.rst for descriptions).\\n240:     Entries should be ordered by those labels (e.g. |Fix| after |Efficiency|).\\n241:     Changes not specific to a module should be listed under *Multiple Modules*\\n242:     or *Miscellaneous*.\\n243:     Entries should end with:\\n244:     :pr:`123456` by :user:`Joe Bloggs <joeongithub>`.\\n245:     where 123456 is the *pull request* number, not the issue number.\\n246: \\n247: :mod:`sklearn.cluster`\\n248: ......................\\n249: \\n250: - |Efficiency| :class:`cluster.Birch` implementation of the predict method\\n251:   avoids high memory footprint by calculating the distances matrix using\\n252:   a chunked scheme.\\n253:   :pr:`16149` by :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>` and\\n254:   :user:`Alex Shacked <alexshacked>`.\\n255: \\n256: - |Efficiency| |MajorFeature| The critical parts of :class:`cluster.KMeans`\\n257:   have a more optimized implementation. Parallelism is now over the data\\n258:   instead of over initializations allowing better scalability. :pr:`11950` by\\n259:   :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n260: \\n261: - |Enhancement| :class:`cluster.KMeans` now supports sparse data when\\n262:   `solver = \\\"elkan\\\"`. :pr:`11950` by\\n263:   :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n264: \\n265: - |Enhancement| :class:`cluster.AgglomerativeClustering` has a faster and more\\n266:   memory efficient implementation of single linkage clustering.\\n267:   :pr:`11514` by :user:`Leland McInnes <lmcinnes>`.\\n268: \\n269: - |Fix| :class:`cluster.KMeans` with ``algorithm=\\\"elkan\\\"`` now converges with\\n270:   ``tol=0`` as with the default ``algorithm=\\\"full\\\"``. :pr:`16075` by\\n271:   :user:`Erich Schubert <kno10>`.\\n272: \\n273: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`cluster.Birch` where the `n_clusters` parameter\\n274:   could not have a `np.int64` type. :pr:`16484`\\n275:   by :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n276: \\n277: - |Fix| :class:`cluster.AgglomerativeClustering` add specific error when\\n278:   distance matrix is not square and `affinity=precomputed`.\\n279:   :pr:`16257` by :user:`Simona Maggio <simonamaggio>`.\\n280: \\n281: - |API| The ``n_jobs`` parameter of :class:`cluster.KMeans`,\\n282:   :class:`cluster.SpectralCoclustering` and\\n283:   :class:`cluster.SpectralBiclustering` is deprecated. They now use OpenMP\\n284:   based parallelism. For more details on how to control the number of threads,\\n285:   please refer to our :ref:`parallelism` notes. :pr:`11950` by\\n286:   :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n287: \\n288: - |API| The ``precompute_distances`` parameter of :class:`cluster.KMeans` is\\n289:   deprecated. It has no effect. :pr:`11950` by\\n290:   :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n291: \\n292: - |API| The ``random_state`` parameter has been added to\\n293:   :class:`cluster.AffinityPropagation`. :pr:`16801` by :user:`rcwoolston`\\n294:   and :user:`Chiara Marmo <cmarmo>`.\\n295: \\n296: :mod:`sklearn.compose`\\n297: ......................\\n298: \\n299: - |Efficiency| :class:`compose.ColumnTransformer` is now faster when working\\n300:   with dataframes and strings are used to specific subsets of data for\\n301:   transformers. :pr:`16431` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n302: \\n303: - |Enhancement| :class:`compose.ColumnTransformer` method ``get_feature_names``\\n304:   now supports `'passthrough'` columns, with the feature name being either\\n305:   the column name for a dataframe, or `'xi'` for column index `i`.\\n306:   :pr:`14048` by :user:`Lewis Ball <lrjball>`.\\n307: \\n308: - |Fix| :class:`compose.ColumnTransformer` method ``get_feature_names`` now\\n309:   returns correct results when one of the transformer steps applies on an\\n310:   empty list of columns :pr:`15963` by `Roman Yurchak`_.\\n311: \\n312: - |Fix| :func:`compose.ColumnTransformer.fit` will error when selecting\\n313:   a column name that is not unique in the dataframe. :pr:`16431` by\\n314:   `Thomas Fan`_.\\n315: \\n316: :mod:`sklearn.datasets`\\n317: .......................\\n318: \\n319: - |Efficiency| :func:`datasets.fetch_openml` has reduced memory usage because\\n320:   it no longer stores the full dataset text stream in memory. :pr:`16084` by\\n321:   `Joel Nothman`_.\\n322: \\n323: - |Feature| :func:`datasets.fetch_california_housing` now supports\\n324:   heterogeneous data using pandas by setting `as_frame=True`. :pr:`15950`\\n325:   by :user:`Stephanie Andrews <gitsteph>` and\\n326:   :user:`Reshama Shaikh <reshamas>`.\\n327: \\n328: - |Feature| embedded dataset loaders :func:`datasets.load_breast_cancer`,\\n329:   :func:`datasets.load_diabetes`, :func:`datasets.load_digits`,\\n330:   :func:`datasets.load_iris`, :func:`datasets.load_linnerud` and\\n331:   :func:`datasets.load_wine` now support loading as a pandas ``DataFrame`` by\\n332:   setting `as_frame=True`. :pr:`15980` by :user:`wconnell` and\\n333:   :user:`Reshama Shaikh <reshamas>`.\\n334: \\n335: - |Enhancement| Added ``return_centers`` parameter  in\\n336:   :func:`datasets.make_blobs`, which can be used to return\\n337:   centers for each cluster.\\n338:   :pr:`15709` by :user:`shivamgargsya` and\\n339:   :user:`Venkatachalam N <venkyyuvy>`.\\n340: \\n341: - |Enhancement| Functions :func:`datasets.make_circles` and\\n342:   :func:`datasets.make_moons` now accept two-element tuple.\\n343:   :pr:`15707` by :user:`Maciej J Mikulski <mjmikulski>`.\\n344: \\n345: - |Fix| :func:`datasets.make_multilabel_classification` now generates\\n346:   `ValueError` for arguments `n_classes < 1` OR `length < 1`.\\n347:   :pr:`16006` by :user:`Rushabh Vasani <rushabh-v>`.\\n348: \\n349: - |API| The `StreamHandler` was removed from `sklearn.logger` to avoid\\n350:   double logging of messages in common cases where a handler is attached\\n351:   to the root logger, and to follow the Python logging documentation\\n352:   recommendation for libraries to leave the log message handling to\\n353:   users and application code. :pr:`16451` by :user:`Christoph Deil <cdeil>`.\\n354: \\n355: :mod:`sklearn.decomposition`\\n356: ............................\\n357: \\n358: - |Enhancement| :class:`decomposition.NMF` and\\n359:   :func:`decomposition.non_negative_factorization` now preserves float32 dtype.\\n360:   :pr:`16280` by :user:`Jeremie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n361: \\n362: - |Enhancement| :func:`decomposition.TruncatedSVD.transform` is now faster on\\n363:   given sparse ``csc`` matrices. :pr:`16837` by :user:`wornbb`.\\n364: \\n365: - |Fix| :class:`decomposition.PCA` with a float `n_components` parameter, will\\n366:   exclusively choose the components that explain the variance greater than\\n367:   `n_components`. :pr:`15669` by :user:`Krishna Chaitanya <krishnachaitanya9>`\\n368: \\n369: - |Fix| :class:`decomposition.PCA` with `n_components='mle'` now correctly\\n370:   handles small eigenvalues, and does not infer 0 as the correct number of\\n371:   components. :pr:`16224` by :user:`Lisa Schwetlick <lschwetlick>`, and\\n372:   :user:`Gelavizh Ahmadi <gelavizh1>` and :user:`Marija Vlajic Wheeler\\n373:   <marijavlajic>` and :pr:`16841` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n374: \\n375: - |Fix| :class:`decomposition.KernelPCA` method ``inverse_transform`` now\\n376:   applies the correct inverse transform to the transformed data. :pr:`16655`\\n377:   by :user:`Lewis Ball <lrjball>`.\\n378: \\n379: - |Fix| Fixed bug that was causing :class:`decomposition.KernelPCA` to sometimes\\n380:   raise `invalid value encountered in multiply` during `fit`.\\n381:   :pr:`16718` by :user:`Gui Miotto <gui-miotto>`.\\n382: \\n383: - |Feature| Added `n_components_` attribute to :class:`decomposition.SparsePCA`\\n384:   and :class:`decomposition.MiniBatchSparsePCA`. :pr:`16981` by\\n385:   :user:`Mateusz Górski <Reksbril>`.\\n386: \\n387: :mod:`sklearn.ensemble`\\n388: .......................\\n389: \\n390: - |MajorFeature|  :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n391:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor` now support\\n392:   :term:`sample_weight`. :pr:`14696` by `Adrin Jalali`_ and `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n393: \\n394: - |Feature| Early stopping in\\n395:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n396:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor` is now determined with a\\n397:   new `early_stopping` parameter instead of `n_iter_no_change`. Default value\\n398:   is 'auto', which enables early stopping if there are at least 10,000\\n399:   samples in the training set. :pr:`14516` by :user:`Johann Faouzi\\n400:   <johannfaouzi>`.\\n401: \\n402: - |MajorFeature| :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n403:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor` now support monotonic\\n404:   constraints, useful when features are supposed to have a positive/negative\\n405:   effect on the target. :pr:`15582` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n406: \\n407: - |API| Added boolean `verbose` flag to classes:\\n408:   :class:`ensemble.VotingClassifier` and :class:`ensemble.VotingRegressor`.\\n409:   :pr:`16069` by :user:`Sam Bail <spbail>`,\\n410:   :user:`Hanna Bruce MacDonald <hannahbrucemacdonald>`,\\n411:   :user:`Reshama Shaikh <reshamas>`, and\\n412:   :user:`Chiara Marmo <cmarmo>`.\\n413: \\n414: - |API| Fixed a bug in :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n415:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor` that would not respect the\\n416:   `max_leaf_nodes` parameter if the criteria was reached at the same time as\\n417:   the `max_depth` criteria. :pr:`16183` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n418: \\n419: - |Fix|  Changed the convention for `max_depth` parameter of\\n420:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n421:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor`. The depth now corresponds to\\n422:   the number of edges to go from the root to the deepest leaf.\\n423:   Stumps (trees with one split) are now allowed.\\n424:   :pr:`16182` by :user:`Santhosh B <santhoshbala18>`\\n425: \\n426: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`ensemble.BaggingClassifier`,\\n427:   :class:`ensemble.BaggingRegressor` and :class:`ensemble.IsolationForest`\\n428:   where the attribute `estimators_samples_` did not generate the proper indices\\n429:   used during `fit`.\\n430:   :pr:`16437` by :user:`Jin-Hwan CHO <chofchof>`.\\n431: \\n432: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`ensemble.StackingClassifier` and\\n433:   :class:`ensemble.StackingRegressor` where the `sample_weight`\\n434:   argument was not being passed to `cross_val_predict` when\\n435:   evaluating the base estimators on cross-validation folds\\n436:   to obtain the input to the meta estimator.\\n437:   :pr:`16539` by :user:`Bill DeRose <wderose>`.\\n438: \\n439: - |Feature| Added additional option `loss=\\\"poisson\\\"` to\\n440:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor`, which adds Poisson deviance\\n441:   with log-link useful for modeling count data.\\n442:   :pr:`16692` by :user:`Christian Lorentzen <lorentzenchr>`\\n443: \\n444: - |Fix| Fixed a bug where :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor` and\\n445:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` would fail with multiple\\n446:   calls to fit when `warm_start=True`, `early_stopping=True`, and there is no\\n447:   validation set. :pr:`16663` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n448: \\n449: :mod:`sklearn.feature_extraction`\\n450: .................................\\n451: \\n452: - |Efficiency| :class:`feature_extraction.text.CountVectorizer` now sorts\\n453:   features after pruning them by document frequency. This improves performances\\n454:   for datasets with large vocabularies combined with ``min_df`` or ``max_df``.\\n455:   :pr:`15834` by :user:`Santiago M. Mola <smola>`.\\n456: \\n457: :mod:`sklearn.feature_selection`\\n458: ................................\\n459: \\n460: - |Enhancement| Added support for multioutput data in\\n461:   :class:`feature_selection.RFE` and :class:`feature_selection.RFECV`.\\n462:   :pr:`16103` by :user:`Divyaprabha M <divyaprabha123>`.\\n463: \\n464: - |API| Adds :class:`feature_selection.SelectorMixin` back to public API.\\n465:   :pr:`16132` by :user:`trimeta`.\\n466: \\n467: :mod:`sklearn.gaussian_process`\\n468: ...............................\\n469: \\n470: - |Enhancement| :func:`gaussian_process.kernels.Matern` returns the RBF kernel when ``nu=np.inf``.\\n471:   :pr:`15503` by :user:`Sam Dixon <sam-dixon>`.\\n472: \\n473: - |Fix| Fixed bug in :class:`gaussian_process.GaussianProcessRegressor` that\\n474:   caused predicted standard deviations to only be between 0 and 1 when\\n475:   WhiteKernel is not used. :pr:`15782`\\n476:   by :user:`plgreenLIRU`.\\n477: \\n478: :mod:`sklearn.impute`\\n479: .....................\\n480: \\n481: - |Enhancement| :class:`impute.IterativeImputer` accepts both scalar and array-like inputs for\\n482:   ``max_value`` and ``min_value``. Array-like inputs allow a different max and min to be specified\\n483:   for each feature. :pr:`16403` by :user:`Narendra Mukherjee <narendramukherjee>`.\\n484: \\n485: - |Enhancement| :class:`impute.SimpleImputer`, :class:`impute.KNNImputer`, and\\n486:   :class:`impute.IterativeImputer` accepts pandas' nullable integer dtype with\\n487:   missing values. :pr:`16508` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n488: \\n489: :mod:`sklearn.inspection`\\n490: .........................\\n491: \\n492: - |Feature| :func:`inspection.partial_dependence` and\\n493:   `inspection.plot_partial_dependence` now support the fast 'recursion'\\n494:   method for :class:`ensemble.RandomForestRegressor` and\\n495:   :class:`tree.DecisionTreeRegressor`. :pr:`15864` by\\n496:   `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n497: \\n498: :mod:`sklearn.linear_model`\\n499: ...........................\\n500: \\n501: - |MajorFeature| Added generalized linear models (GLM) with non normal error\\n502:   distributions, including :class:`linear_model.PoissonRegressor`,\\n503:   :class:`linear_model.GammaRegressor` and :class:`linear_model.TweedieRegressor`\\n504:   which use Poisson, Gamma and Tweedie distributions respectively.\\n505:   :pr:`14300` by :user:`Christian Lorentzen <lorentzenchr>`, `Roman Yurchak`_,\\n506:   and `Olivier Grisel`_.\\n507: \\n508: - |MajorFeature| Support of `sample_weight` in\\n509:   :class:`linear_model.ElasticNet` and :class:`linear_model.Lasso` for dense\\n510:   feature matrix `X`. :pr:`15436` by :user:`Christian Lorentzen\\n511:   <lorentzenchr>`.\\n512: \\n513: - |Efficiency| :class:`linear_model.RidgeCV` and\\n514:   :class:`linear_model.RidgeClassifierCV` now does not allocate a\\n515:   potentially large array to store dual coefficients for all hyperparameters\\n516:   during its `fit`, nor an array to store all error or LOO predictions unless\\n517:   `store_cv_values` is `True`.\\n518:   :pr:`15652` by :user:`Jérôme Dockès <jeromedockes>`.\\n519: \\n520: - |Enhancement| :class:`linear_model.LassoLars` and\\n521:   :class:`linear_model.Lars` now support a `jitter` parameter that adds\\n522:   random noise to the target. This might help with stability in some edge\\n523:   cases. :pr:`15179` by :user:`angelaambroz`.\\n524: \\n525: - |Fix| Fixed a bug where if a `sample_weight` parameter was passed to the fit\\n526:   method of :class:`linear_model.RANSACRegressor`, it would not be passed to\\n527:   the wrapped `base_estimator` during the fitting of the final model.\\n528:   :pr:`15773` by :user:`Jeremy Alexandre <J-A16>`.\\n529: \\n530: - |Fix| Add `best_score_` attribute to :class:`linear_model.RidgeCV` and\\n531:   :class:`linear_model.RidgeClassifierCV`.\\n532:   :pr:`15655` by :user:`Jérôme Dockès <jeromedockes>`.\\n533: \\n534: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`linear_model.RidgeClassifierCV` to pass a\\n535:   specific scoring strategy. Before the internal estimator outputs score\\n536:   instead of predictions.\\n537:   :pr:`14848` by :user:`Venkatachalam N <venkyyuvy>`.\\n538: \\n539: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegression` will now avoid an unnecessary\\n540:   iteration when `solver='newton-cg'` by checking for inferior or equal instead\\n541:   of strictly inferior for maximum of `absgrad` and `tol` in `utils.optimize._newton_cg`.\\n542:   :pr:`16266` by :user:`Rushabh Vasani <rushabh-v>`.\\n543: \\n544: - |API| Deprecated public attributes `standard_coef_`, `standard_intercept_`,\\n545:   `average_coef_`, and `average_intercept_` in\\n546:   :class:`linear_model.SGDClassifier`,\\n547:   :class:`linear_model.SGDRegressor`,\\n548:   :class:`linear_model.PassiveAggressiveClassifier`,\\n549:   :class:`linear_model.PassiveAggressiveRegressor`.\\n550:   :pr:`16261` by :user:`Carlos Brandt <chbrandt>`.\\n551: \\n552: - |Fix| |Efficiency| :class:`linear_model.ARDRegression` is more stable and\\n553:   much faster when `n_samples > n_features`. It can now scale to hundreds of\\n554:   thousands of samples. The stability fix might imply changes in the number\\n555:   of non-zero coefficients and in the predicted output. :pr:`16849` by\\n556:   `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n557: \\n558: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :class:`linear_model.ElasticNetCV`,\\n559:   :class:`linear_model.MultiTaskElasticNetCV`, :class:`linear_model.LassoCV`\\n560:   and :class:`linear_model.MultiTaskLassoCV` where fitting would fail when\\n561:   using joblib loky backend. :pr:`14264` by\\n562:   :user:`Jérémie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>`.\\n563: \\n564: - |Efficiency| Speed up :class:`linear_model.MultiTaskLasso`,\\n565:   :class:`linear_model.MultiTaskLassoCV`, :class:`linear_model.MultiTaskElasticNet`,\\n566:   :class:`linear_model.MultiTaskElasticNetCV` by avoiding slower\\n567:   BLAS Level 2 calls on small arrays\\n568:   :pr:`17021` by :user:`Alex Gramfort <agramfort>` and\\n569:   :user:`Mathurin Massias <mathurinm>`.\\n570: \\n571: :mod:`sklearn.metrics`\\n572: ......................\\n573: \\n574: - |Enhancement| :func:`metrics.pairwise_distances_chunked` now allows\\n575:   its ``reduce_func`` to not have a return value, enabling in-place operations.\\n576:   :pr:`16397` by `Joel Nothman`_.\\n577: \\n578: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :func:`metrics.mean_squared_error` to not ignore\\n579:   argument `squared` when argument `multioutput='raw_values'`.\\n580:   :pr:`16323` by :user:`Rushabh Vasani <rushabh-v>`\\n581: \\n582: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :func:`metrics.mutual_info_score` where negative\\n583:   scores could be returned. :pr:`16362` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n584: \\n585: - |Fix| Fixed a bug in :func:`metrics.confusion_matrix` that would raise\\n586:   an error when `y_true` and `y_pred` were length zero and `labels` was\\n587:   not `None`. In addition, we raise an error when an empty list is given to\\n588:   the `labels` parameter.\\n589:   :pr:`16442` by :user:`Kyle Parsons <parsons-kyle-89>`.\\n590: \\n591: - |API| Changed the formatting of values in\\n592:   :meth:`metrics.ConfusionMatrixDisplay.plot` and\\n593:   `metrics.plot_confusion_matrix` to pick the shorter format (either '2g'\\n594:   or 'd'). :pr:`16159` by :user:`Rick Mackenbach <Rick-Mackenbach>` and\\n595:   `Thomas Fan`_.\\n596: \\n597: - |API| From version 0.25, :func:`metrics.pairwise_distances` will no\\n598:   longer automatically compute the ``VI`` parameter for Mahalanobis distance\\n599:   and the ``V`` parameter for seuclidean distance if ``Y`` is passed. The user\\n600:   will be expected to compute this parameter on the training data of their\\n601:   choice and pass it to `pairwise_distances`. :pr:`16993` by `Joel Nothman`_.\\n602: \\n603: :mod:`sklearn.model_selection`\\n604: ..............................\\n605: \\n606: - |Enhancement| :class:`model_selection.GridSearchCV` and\\n607:   :class:`model_selection.RandomizedSearchCV` yields stack trace information\\n608:   in fit failed warning messages in addition to previously emitted\\n609:   type and details.\\n610:   :pr:`15622` by :user:`Gregory Morse <GregoryMorse>`.\\n611: \\n612: - |Fix| :func:`model_selection.cross_val_predict` supports\\n613:   `method=\\\"predict_proba\\\"` when `y=None`. :pr:`15918` by\\n614:   :user:`Luca Kubin <lkubin>`.\\n615: \\n616: - |Fix| `model_selection.fit_grid_point` is deprecated in 0.23 and will\\n617:   be removed in 0.25. :pr:`16401` by\\n618:   :user:`Arie Pratama Sutiono <ariepratama>`\\n619: \\n620: :mod:`sklearn.multioutput`\\n621: ..........................\\n622: \\n623: - |Feature| :func:`multioutput.MultiOutputRegressor.fit` and\\n624:   :func:`multioutput.MultiOutputClassifier.fit` now can accept `fit_params`\\n625:   to pass to the `estimator.fit` method of each step. :issue:`15953`\\n626:   :pr:`15959` by :user:`Ke Huang <huangk10>`.\\n627: \\n628: - |Enhancement| :class:`multioutput.RegressorChain` now supports `fit_params`\\n629:   for `base_estimator` during `fit`.\\n630:   :pr:`16111` by :user:`Venkatachalam N <venkyyuvy>`.\\n631: \\n632: :mod:`sklearn.naive_bayes`\\n633: .............................\\n634: \\n635: - |Fix| A correctly formatted error message is shown in\\n636:   :class:`naive_bayes.CategoricalNB` when the number of features in the input\\n637:   differs between `predict` and `fit`.\\n638:   :pr:`16090` by :user:`Madhura Jayaratne <madhuracj>`.\\n639: \\n640: :mod:`sklearn.neural_network`\\n641: .............................\\n642: \\n643: - |Efficiency| :class:`neural_network.MLPClassifier` and\\n644:   :class:`neural_network.MLPRegressor` has reduced memory footprint when using\\n645:   stochastic solvers, `'sgd'` or `'adam'`, and `shuffle=True`. :pr:`14075` by\\n646:   :user:`meyer89`.\\n647: \\n648: - |Fix| Increases the numerical stability of the logistic loss function in\\n649:   :class:`neural_network.MLPClassifier` by clipping the probabilities.\\n650:   :pr:`16117` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n651: \\n652: :mod:`sklearn.inspection`\\n653: .........................\\n654: \\n655: - |Enhancement| :class:`inspection.PartialDependenceDisplay` now exposes the\\n656:   deciles lines as attributes so they can be hidden or customized. :pr:`15785`\\n657:   by `Nicolas Hug`_\\n658: \\n659: :mod:`sklearn.preprocessing`\\n660: ............................\\n661: \\n662: - |Feature| argument `drop` of :class:`preprocessing.OneHotEncoder`\\n663:   will now accept value 'if_binary' and will drop the first category of\\n664:   each feature with two categories. :pr:`16245`\\n665:   by :user:`Rushabh Vasani <rushabh-v>`.\\n666: \\n667: - |Enhancement| :class:`preprocessing.OneHotEncoder`'s `drop_idx_` ndarray\\n668:   can now contain `None`, where `drop_idx_[i] = None` means that no category\\n669:   is dropped for index `i`. :pr:`16585` by :user:`Chiara Marmo <cmarmo>`.\\n670: \\n671: - |Enhancement| :class:`preprocessing.MaxAbsScaler`,\\n672:   :class:`preprocessing.MinMaxScaler`, :class:`preprocessing.StandardScaler`,\\n673:   :class:`preprocessing.PowerTransformer`,\\n674:   :class:`preprocessing.QuantileTransformer`,\\n675:   :class:`preprocessing.RobustScaler` now supports pandas' nullable integer\\n676:   dtype with missing values. :pr:`16508` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n677: \\n678: - |Efficiency| :class:`preprocessing.OneHotEncoder` is now faster at\\n679:   transforming. :pr:`15762` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n680: \\n681: - |Fix| Fix a bug in :class:`preprocessing.StandardScaler` which was incorrectly\\n682:   computing statistics when calling `partial_fit` on sparse inputs.\\n683:   :pr:`16466` by :user:`Guillaume Lemaitre <glemaitre>`.\\n684: \\n685: - |Fix| Fix a bug in :class:`preprocessing.Normalizer` with norm='max',\\n686:   which was not taking the absolute value of the maximum values before\\n687:   normalizing the vectors. :pr:`16632` by\\n688:   :user:`Maura Pintor <Maupin1991>` and :user:`Battista Biggio <bbiggio>`.\\n689: \\n690: :mod:`sklearn.semi_supervised`\\n691: ..............................\\n692: \\n693: - |Fix| :class:`semi_supervised.LabelSpreading` and\\n694:   :class:`semi_supervised.LabelPropagation` avoids divide by zero warnings\\n695:   when normalizing `label_distributions_`. :pr:`15946` by :user:`ngshya`.\\n696: \\n697: :mod:`sklearn.svm`\\n698: ..................\\n699: \\n700: - |Fix| |Efficiency| Improved ``libsvm`` and ``liblinear`` random number\\n701:   generators used to randomly select coordinates in the coordinate descent\\n702:   algorithms. Platform-dependent C ``rand()`` was used, which is only able to\\n703:   generate numbers up to ``32767`` on windows platform (see this `blog\\n704:   post <https://codeforces.com/blog/entry/61587>`_) and also has poor\\n705:   randomization power as suggested by `this presentation\\n706:   <https://channel9.msdn.com/Events/GoingNative/2013/rand-Considered-Harmful>`_.\\n707:   It was replaced with C++11 ``mt19937``, a Mersenne Twister that correctly\\n708:   generates 31bits/63bits random numbers on all platforms. In addition, the\\n709:   crude \\\"modulo\\\" postprocessor used to get a random number in a bounded\\n710:   interval was replaced by the tweaked Lemire method as suggested by `this blog\\n711:   post <http://www.pcg-random.org/posts/bounded-rands.html>`_.\\n712:   Any model using the `svm.libsvm` or the `svm.liblinear` solver,\\n713:   including :class:`svm.LinearSVC`, :class:`svm.LinearSVR`,\\n714:   :class:`svm.NuSVC`, :class:`svm.NuSVR`, :class:`svm.OneClassSVM`,\\n715:   :class:`svm.SVC`, :class:`svm.SVR`, :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegression`,\\n716:   is affected. In particular users can expect a better convergence when the\\n717:   number of samples (LibSVM) or the number of features (LibLinear) is large.\\n718:   :pr:`13511` by :user:`Sylvain Marié <smarie>`.\\n719: \\n720: - |Fix| Fix use of custom kernel not taking float entries such as string\\n721:   kernels in :class:`svm.SVC` and :class:`svm.SVR`. Note that custom kennels\\n722:   are now expected to validate their input where they previously received\\n723:   valid numeric arrays.\\n724:   :pr:`11296` by `Alexandre Gramfort`_ and  :user:`Georgi Peev <georgipeev>`.\\n725: \\n726: - |API| :class:`svm.SVR` and :class:`svm.OneClassSVM` attributes, `probA_` and\\n727:   `probB_`, are now deprecated as they were not useful. :pr:`15558` by\\n728:   `Thomas Fan`_.\\n729: \\n730: :mod:`sklearn.tree`\\n731: ...................\\n732: \\n733: - |Fix| :func:`tree.plot_tree` `rotate` parameter was unused and has been\\n734:   deprecated.\\n735:   :pr:`15806` by :user:`Chiara Marmo <cmarmo>`.\\n736: \\n737: - |Fix| Fix support of read-only float32 array input in ``predict``,\\n738:   ``decision_path`` and ``predict_proba`` methods of\\n739:   :class:`tree.DecisionTreeClassifier`, :class:`tree.ExtraTreeClassifier` and\\n740:   :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier` as well as ``predict`` method of\\n741:   :class:`tree.DecisionTreeRegressor`, :class:`tree.ExtraTreeRegressor`, and\\n742:   :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingRegressor`.\\n743:   :pr:`16331` by :user:`Alexandre Batisse <batalex>`.\\n744: \\n745: :mod:`sklearn.utils`\\n746: ....................\\n747: \\n748: - |MajorFeature| Estimators can now be displayed with a rich html\\n749:   representation. This can be enabled in Jupyter notebooks by setting\\n750:   `display='diagram'` in :func:`~sklearn.set_config`. The raw html can be\\n751:   returned by using :func:`utils.estimator_html_repr`.\\n752:   :pr:`14180` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n753: \\n754: - |Enhancement| improve error message in :func:`utils.validation.column_or_1d`.\\n755:   :pr:`15926` by :user:`Loïc Estève <lesteve>`.\\n756: \\n757: - |Enhancement| add warning in :func:`utils.check_array` for\\n758:   pandas sparse DataFrame.\\n759:   :pr:`16021` by :user:`Rushabh Vasani <rushabh-v>`.\\n760: \\n761: - |Enhancement| :func:`utils.check_array` now constructs a sparse\\n762:   matrix from a pandas DataFrame that contains only `SparseArray` columns.\\n763:   :pr:`16728` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n764: \\n765: - |Enhancement| :func:`utils.check_array` supports pandas'\\n766:   nullable integer dtype with missing values when `force_all_finite` is set to\\n767:   `False` or `'allow-nan'` in which case the data is converted to floating\\n768:   point values where `pd.NA` values are replaced by `np.nan`. As a consequence,\\n769:   all :mod:`sklearn.preprocessing` transformers that accept numeric inputs with\\n770:   missing values represented as `np.nan` now also accepts being directly fed\\n771:   pandas dataframes with `pd.Int* or `pd.Uint*` typed columns that use `pd.NA`\\n772:   as a missing value marker. :pr:`16508` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n773: \\n774: - |API| Passing classes to :func:`utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` and\\n775:   :func:`utils.estimator_checks.parametrize_with_checks` is now deprecated,\\n776:   and support for classes will be removed in 0.24. Pass instances instead.\\n777:   :pr:`17032` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n778: \\n779: - |API| The private utility `_safe_tags` in `utils.estimator_checks` was\\n780:   removed, hence all tags should be obtained through `estimator._get_tags()`.\\n781:   Note that Mixins like `RegressorMixin` must come *before* base classes\\n782:   in the MRO for `_get_tags()` to work properly.\\n783:   :pr:`16950` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n784: \\n785: - |FIX| `utils.all_estimators` now only returns public estimators.\\n786:   :pr:`15380` by `Thomas Fan`_.\\n787: \\n788: Miscellaneous\\n789: .............\\n790: \\n791: - |MajorFeature| Adds a HTML representation of estimators to be shown in\\n792:   a jupyter notebook or lab. This visualization is activated by setting the\\n793:   `display` option in :func:`sklearn.set_config`. :pr:`14180` by\\n794:   `Thomas Fan`_.\\n795: \\n796: - |Enhancement| ``scikit-learn`` now works with ``mypy`` without errors.\\n797:   :pr:`16726` by `Roman Yurchak`_.\\n798: \\n799: - |API| Most estimators now expose a `n_features_in_` attribute. This\\n800:   attribute is equal to the number of features passed to the `fit` method.\\n801:   See `SLEP010\\n802:   <https://scikit-learn-enhancement-proposals.readthedocs.io/en/latest/slep010/proposal.html>`_\\n803:   for details. :pr:`16112` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n804: \\n805: - |API| Estimators now have a `requires_y` tags which is False by default\\n806:   except for estimators that inherit from `~sklearn.base.RegressorMixin` or\\n807:   `~sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`. This tag is used to ensure that a proper\\n808:   error message is raised when y was expected but None was passed.\\n809:   :pr:`16622` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n810: \\n811: - |API| The default setting `print_changed_only` has been changed from False\\n812:   to True. This means that the `repr` of estimators is now more concise and\\n813:   only shows the parameters whose default value has been changed when\\n814:   printing an estimator. You can restore the previous behaviour by using\\n815:   `sklearn.set_config(print_changed_only=False)`. Also, note that it is\\n816:   always possible to quickly inspect the parameters of any estimator using\\n817:   `est.get_params(deep=False)`. :pr:`17061` by `Nicolas Hug`_.\\n818: \\n819: .. rubric:: Code and documentation contributors\\n820: \\n821: Thanks to everyone who has contributed to the maintenance and improvement of the\\n822: project since version 0.22, including:\\n823: \\n824: Abbie Popa, Adrin Jalali, Aleksandra Kocot, Alexandre Batisse, Alexandre\\n825: Gramfort, Alex Henrie, Alex Itkes, Alex Liang, alexshacked, Alonso Silva\\n826: Allende, Ana Casado, Andreas Mueller, Angela Ambroz, Ankit810, Arie Pratama\\n827: Sutiono, Arunav Konwar, Baptiste Maingret, Benjamin Beier Liu, bernie gray,\\n828: Bharathi Srinivasan, Bharat Raghunathan, Bibhash Chandra Mitra, Brian Wignall,\\n829: brigi, Brigitta Sipőcz, Carlos H Brandt, CastaChick, castor, cgsavard, Chiara\\n830: Marmo, Chris Gregory, Christian Kastner, Christian Lorentzen, Corrie\\n831: Bartelheimer, Daniël van Gelder, Daphne, David Breuer, david-cortes, dbauer9,\\n832: Divyaprabha M, Edward Qian, Ekaterina Borovikova, ELNS, Emily Taylor, Erich\\n833: Schubert, Eric Leung, Evgeni Chasnovski, Fabiana, Facundo Ferrín, Fan,\\n834: Franziska Boenisch, Gael Varoquaux, Gaurav Sharma, Geoffrey Bolmier, Georgi\\n835: Peev, gholdman1, Gonthier Nicolas, Gregory Morse, Gregory R. By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`30451`\\n52: \\n53: :mod:`sklearn.tree`\\n54: -------------------\\n55: \\n56: - |Fix| Use `log2` instead of `ln` for building trees to maintain behavior of previous\\n57:   versions. By `Thomas Fan`_ :pr:`30557`\\n58: \\n59: :mod:`sklearn.utils`\\n60: --------------------\\n61: \\n62: - |Enhancement| :func:`utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator_sparse_tag` ensures that\\n63:   the estimator tag `input_tags.sparse` is consistent with its `fit`\\n64:   method (accepting sparse input `X` or raising the appropriate error).\\n65:   By :user:`Antoine Baker <antoinebaker>` :pr:`30187`\\n66: \\n67: - |Fix| Raise a `DeprecationWarning` when there is no concrete implementation of `__sklearn_tags__`\\n68:   in the MRO of the estimator. We request to inherit from `BaseEstimator` that\\n69:   implements `__sklearn_tags__`.\\n70:   By :user:`Guillaume Lemaitre <glemaitre>` :pr:`30516`\\n71: \\n72: .. _changes_1_6_0:\\n73: \\n74: Version 1.6.0\\n75: =============\\n76: \\n77: **December 2024**\\n78: \\n79: Changes impacting many modules\\n80: ------------------------------\\n81: \\n82: - |Enhancement| `__sklearn_tags__` was introduced for setting tags in estimators.\\n83:   More details in :ref:`estimator_tags`.\\n84:   By :user:`Thomas Fan <thomasjpfan>` and :user:`Adrin Jalali <adrinjalali>` :pr:`29677`\\n85: \\n86: - |Enhancement| Scikit-learn classes and functions can be used while only having a\\n87:   `import sklearn` import line. For example, `import sklearn; sklearn.svm.SVC()` now works.\\n88:   By :user:`Thomas Fan <thomasjpfan>` :pr:`29793`\\n89: \\n90: - |Fix| Classes :class:`metrics.ConfusionMatrixDisplay`,\\n91:   :class:`metrics.RocCurveDisplay`, :class:`calibration.CalibrationDisplay`,\\n92:   :class:`metrics.PrecisionRecallDisplay`, :class:`metrics.PredictionErrorDisplay` and\\n93:   :class:`inspection.PartialDependenceDisplay` now properly handle Matplotlib aliases\\n94:   for style parameters (e.g., `c` and `color`, `ls` and `linestyle`, etc).\\n95:   By :user:`Joseph Barbier <JosephBARBIERDARNAL>` :pr:`30023`\\n96: \\n97: - |API| :func:`utils.validation.validate_data` is introduced and replaces previously\\n98:   private `base.BaseEstimator._validate_data` method. This is intended for third party\\n99:   estimator developers, who should use this function in most cases instead of\\n100:   :func:`utils.check_array` and :func:`utils.check_X_y`.\\n101:   By :user:`Adrin Jalali <adrinjalali>` :pr:`29696`\\n102: \\n103: Support for Array API\\n104: ---------------------\\n105: \\n106: Additional estimators and functions have been updated to include support for all\\n107: `Array API <https://data-apis.org/array-api/latest/>`_ compliant inputs.\\n108: \\n109: See :ref:`array_api` for more details.\\n110: \\n111: - |Feature| :class:`model_selection.GridSearchCV`,\\n112:   :class:`model_selection.RandomizedSearchCV`,\\n113:   :class:`model_selection.HalvingGridSearchCV` and\\n114:   :class:`model_selection.HalvingRandomSearchCV` now support Array API\\n115:   compatible inputs when their base estimators do.\\n116:   By :user:`Tim Head <betatim>` and :user:`Olivier Grisel <ogrisel>` :pr:`27096`\\n117: \\n118: - |Feature| :func:`sklearn.metrics.f1_score` now supports Array API compatible\\n119:   inputs.\\n120:   By :user:`Omar Salman <OmarManzoor>` :pr:`27369`\\n121: \\n122: - |Feature| :class:`preprocessing.LabelEncoder` now supports Array API compatible inputs.\\n123:   By :user:`Omar Salman <OmarManzoor>` :pr:`27381`\\n124: \\n125: - |Feature| :func:`sklearn.metrics.pairwise.chi2_kernel` now supports Array API\\n173:   compatible inputs.\\n174:   By :user:`Yaroslav Korobko <Tialo>` :pr:`29267`\\n175: \\n176: - |Feature| :func:`sklearn.metrics.mean_absolute_percentage_error` now supports Array API\\n177:   compatible inputs.\\n178:   By :user:`Emily Chen <EmilyXinyi>` :pr:`29300`\\n179: \\n180: - |Feature| :func:`sklearn.metrics.pairwise.paired_euclidean_distances` now supports\\n181:   Array API compatible inputs.\\n182:   By :user:`Emily Chen <EmilyXinyi>` :pr:`29389`\\n183: \\n184: - |Feature| :func:`sklearn.metrics.pairwise.euclidean_distances` and\\n185:   :func:`sklearn.metrics.pairwise.rbf_kernel` now supports Array API compatible\\n186:   inputs.\\n187:   By :user:`Omar Salman <OmarManzoor>` :pr:`29433`\\n188: \\n189: - |Feature| :func:`sklearn.metrics.pairwise.linear_kernel`,\\n190:   :func:`sklearn.metrics.pairwise.sigmoid_kernel`, and\\n191:   :func:`sklearn.metrics.pairwise.polynomial_kernel` now supports Array API\\n192:   compatible inputs.\\n193:   By :user:`Omar Salman <OmarManzoor>` :pr:`29475`\\n194: \\n195: - |Feature| :func:`sklearn.metrics.mean_squared_log_error` and\\n196:   :func:`sklearn.metrics.root_mean_squared_log_error`\\n197:   now supports Array API compatible inputs.\\n198:   By :user:`Virgil Chan <virchan>` :pr:`29709`\\n199: \\n200: - |Feature| :class:`preprocessing.MinMaxScaler` with `clip=True` now supports Array API\\n201:   compatible inputs.\\n202:   By :user:`Shreekant Nandiyawar <Shree7676>` :pr:`29751`\\n203: \\n204: - Support for the soon to be deprecated `cupy.array_api` module has been\\n205:   removed in favor of directly supporting the top level `cupy` module, possibly\\n206:   via the `array_api_compat.cupy` compatibility wrapper.\\n207:   By :user:`Olivier Grisel <ogrisel>` :pr:`29639`\\n208: \\n209: Metadata routing\\n210: ----------------\\n211: \\n212: Refer to the :ref:`Metadata Routing User Guide <metadata_routing>` for\\n213: more details.\\n214: \\n215: - |Feature| :class:`semi_supervised.SelfTrainingClassifier`\\n216:   now supports metadata routing. The fit method now accepts ``**fit_params``\\n217:   which are passed to the underlying estimators via their `fit` methods.\\n218:   In addition, the\\n219:   :meth:`~semi_supervised.SelfTrainingClassifier.predict`,\\n220:   :meth:`~semi_supervised.SelfTrainingClassifier.predict_proba`,\\n221:   :meth:`~semi_supervised.SelfTrainingClassifier.predict_log_proba`,\\n222:   :meth:`~semi_supervised.SelfTrainingClassifier.score`\\n223:   and :meth:`~semi_supervised.SelfTrainingClassifier.decision_function`\\n224:   methods also accept ``**params`` which are\\n225:   passed to the underlying estimators via their respective methods.\\n226:   By :user:`Adam Li <adam2392>` :pr:`28494`\\n227: \\n228: - |Feature| :class:`ensemble.StackingClassifier` and\\n229:   :class:`ensemble.StackingRegressor` now support metadata routing and pass\\n230:   ``**fit_params`` to the underlying estimators via their `fit` methods.\\n231:   By :user:`Stefanie Senger <StefanieSenger>` :pr:`28701`\\n232: \\n233: - |Feature| :func:`model_selection.learning_curve` now supports metadata routing for the\\n234:   `fit` method of its estimator and for its underlying CV splitter and scorer.\\n235:   By :user:`Stefanie Senger <StefanieSenger>` :pr:`28975`\\n236: \\n237: - |Feature| :class:`compose.TransformedTargetRegressor` now supports metadata\\n238:   routing in its :meth:`~compose.TransformedTargetRegressor.fit` and\\n239:   :meth:`~compose.TransformedTargetRegressor.predict` methods and routes the\\n240:   corresponding params to the underlying regressor.\\n241:   By :user:`Omar Salman <OmarManzoor>` :pr:`29136`\\n242: \\n243: - |Feature| :class:`feature_selection.SequentialFeatureSelector` now supports\\n244:   metadata routing in its `fit` method and passes the corresponding params to\\n245:   the :func:`model_selection.cross_val_score` function.\\n246:   By :user:`Omar Salman <OmarManzoor>` :pr:`29260`\\n247: \\n248: - |Feature| :func:`model_selection.permutation_test_score` now supports metadata routing\\n249:   for the `fit` method of its estimator and for its underlying CV splitter and scorer.\\n250:   By :user:`Adam Li <adam2392>` :pr:`29266`\\n251: \\n252: - |Feature| :class:`feature_selection.RFE` and :class:`feature_selection.RFECV`\\n253:   now support metadata routing.\\n254:   By :user:`Omar Salman <OmarManzoor>` :pr:`29312`\\n255: \\n256: - |Feature| :func:`model_selection.validation_curve` now supports metadata routing for\\n257:   the `fit` method of its estimator and for its underlying CV splitter and scorer.\\n258:   By :user:`Stefanie Senger <StefanieSenger>` :pr:`29329`\\n259: \\n260: - |Fix| Metadata is routed correctly to grouped CV splitters via\\n261:   :class:`linear_model.RidgeCV` and :class:`linear_model.RidgeClassifierCV` and\\n262:   `UnsetMetadataPassedError` is fixed for :class:`linear_model.RidgeClassifierCV` with\\n263:   default scoring.\\n264:   By :user:`Stefanie Senger <StefanieSenger>` :pr:`29634`\\n265: \\n266: - |Fix| Many method arguments which shouldn't be included in the routing mechanism are\\n267:   now excluded and the `set_{method}_request` methods are not generated for them.\\n268:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`29920`\\n269: \\n270: Dropping official support for PyPy\\n271: ----------------------------------\\n272: \\n273: Due to limited maintainer resources and small number of users, official PyPy\\n274: support has been dropped. Some parts of scikit-learn may still work but PyPy is\\n275: not tested anymore in the scikit-learn Continuous Integration.\\n276: By :user:`Loïc Estève <lesteve>` :pr:`29128`\\n277: \\n278: Dropping support for building with setuptools\\n279: ---------------------------------------------\\n280: \\n281: From scikit-learn 1.6 onwards, support for building with setuptools has been\\n282: removed. Meson is the only supported way to build scikit-learn, see\\n283: :ref:`Building from source <install_bleeding_edge>` for more details.\\n284: By :user:`Loïc Estève <lesteve>` :pr:`29400`\\n285: \\n286: Free-threaded CPython 3.13 support\\n287: ----------------------------------\\n288: \\n289: scikit-learn has preliminary support for free-threaded CPython, in particular\\n290: free-threaded wheels are available for all of our supported platforms.\\n291: \\n292: Free-threaded (also known as nogil) CPython 3.13 is an experimental version of\\n293: CPython 3.13 who aims at enabling efficient multi-threaded use cases by\\n294: removing the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL).\\n295: \\n296: For more details about free-threaded CPython see `py-free-threading doc <https://py-free-threading.github.io>`_,\\n297: in particular `how to install a free-threaded CPython <https://py-free-threading.github.io/installing_cpython/>`_\\n298: and `Ecosystem compatibility tracking <https://py-free-threading.github.io/tracking/>`_.\\n299: \\n300: Feel free to try free-threaded on your use case and report any issues!\\n301: \\n302: By :user:`Loïc Estève <lesteve>` and many other people in the wider Scientific\\n303: Python and CPython ecosystem, for example :user:`Nathan Goldbaum <ngoldbaum>`,\\n304: :user:`Ralf Gommers <rgommers>`, :user:`Edgar Andrés Margffoy Tuay <andfoy>`. :pr:`30360`\\n305: \\n306: :mod:`sklearn.base`\\n307: -------------------\\n308: \\n309: - |Enhancement| Added a function :func:`base.is_clusterer` which determines whether a given\\n310:   estimator is of category clusterer.\\n311:   By :user:`Christian Veenhuis <ChVeen>` :pr:`28936`\\n312: \\n313: - |API| Passing a class object to :func:`~sklearn.base.is_classifier`,\\n314:   :func:`~sklearn.base.is_regressor`, and\\n315:   :func:`~sklearn.base.is_outlier_detector` is now deprecated. Pass an instance\\n316:   instead.\\n317:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`30122`\\n318: \\n319: :mod:`sklearn.calibration`\\n320: --------------------------\\n321: \\n322: - |API| `cv=\\\"prefit\\\"` is deprecated for :class:`~sklearn.calibration.CalibratedClassifierCV`.\\n323:   Use :class:`~sklearn.frozen.FrozenEstimator` instead, as\\n324:   `CalibratedClassifierCV(FrozenEstimator(estimator))`.\\n325:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`30171`\\n326: \\n327: :mod:`sklearn.cluster`\\n328: ----------------------\\n329: \\n330: - |API| The `copy` parameter of :class:`cluster.Birch` was deprecated in 1.6 and will be\\n331:   removed in 1.8. It has no effect as the estimator does not perform in-place operations\\n332:   on the input data.\\n333:   By :user:`Yao Xiao <Charlie-XIAO>` :pr:`29124`\\n334: \\n335: :mod:`sklearn.compose`\\n336: ----------------------\\n337: \\n338: - |Enhancement| :func:`sklearn.compose.ColumnTransformer` `verbose_feature_names_out`\\n339:   now accepts string format or callable to generate feature names.\\n340:   By :user:`Marc Bresson <MarcBresson>` :pr:`28934`\\n341: \\n342: :mod:`sklearn.covariance`\\n343: -------------------------\\n344: \\n345: - |Efficiency| :class:`covariance.MinCovDet` fitting is now slightly faster.\\n346:   By :user:`Antony Lee <anntzer>` :pr:`29835`\\n347: \\n348: :mod:`sklearn.cross_decomposition`\\n349: ----------------------------------\\n350: \\n351: - |Fix| :class:`cross_decomposition.PLSRegression` properly raises an error when\\n352:   `n_components` is larger than `n_samples`.\\n353:   By :user:`Thomas Fan <thomasjpfan>` :pr:`29710`\\n354: \\n355: :mod:`sklearn.datasets`\\n356: -----------------------\\n357: \\n358: - |Feature| :func:`datasets.fetch_file` allows downloading arbitrary data-file\\n359:   from the web. It handles local caching, integrity checks with SHA256 digests\\n360:   and automatic retries in case of HTTP errors.\\n361:   By :user:`Olivier Grisel <ogrisel>` :pr:`29354`\\n362: \\n363: :mod:`sklearn.decomposition`\\n364: ----------------------------\\n365: \\n366: - |Enhancement| :class:`~sklearn.decomposition.LatentDirichletAllocation` now has a\\n367:   ``normalize`` parameter in\\n368:   :meth:`~sklearn.decomposition.LatentDirichletAllocation.transform` and\\n369:   :meth:`~sklearn.decomposition.LatentDirichletAllocation.fit_transform`\\n370:   methods to control whether the document topic distribution is normalized.\\n371:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`30097`\\n372: \\n373: - |Fix| :class:`~sklearn.decomposition.IncrementalPCA`\\n374:   will now only raise a ``ValueError`` when the number of samples in the\\n375:   input data to ``partial_fit`` is less than the number of components\\n376:   on the first call to ``partial_fit``. Subsequent calls to ``partial_fit``\\n377:   no longer face this restriction.\\n378:   By :user:`Thomas Gessey-Jones <ThomasGesseyJonesPX>` :pr:`30224`\\n379: \\n380: :mod:`sklearn.discriminant_analysis`\\n381: ------------------------------------\\n382: \\n383: - |Fix| :class:`discriminant_analysis.QuadraticDiscriminantAnalysis`\\n384:   will now cause `LinAlgWarning` in case of collinear variables. These errors\\n385:   can be silenced using the `reg_param` attribute.\\n386:   By :user:`Alihan Zihna <azihna>` :pr:`19731`\\n387: \\n388: :mod:`sklearn.ensemble`\\n389: -----------------------\\n390: \\n391: - |Feature| :class:`ensemble.ExtraTreesClassifier` and\\n392:   :class:`ensemble.ExtraTreesRegressor` now support missing-values in the data matrix\\n393:   `X`. Missing-values are handled by randomly moving all of the samples to the left, or\\n394:   right child node as the tree is traversed.\\n395:   By :user:`Adam Li <adam2392>` :pr:`28268`\\n396: \\n397: - |Efficiency| Small runtime improvement of fitting\\n398:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier` and\\n399:   :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor` by parallelizing the initial search\\n400:   for bin thresholds.\\n401:   By :user:`Christian Lorentzen <lorentzenchr>` :pr:`28064`\\n402: \\n403: - |Efficiency| :class:`ensemble.IsolationForest` now runs parallel jobs\\n404:   during :term:`predict` offering a speedup of up to 2-4x on sample sizes\\n405:   larger than 2000 using `joblib`.\\n406:   By :user:`Adam Li <adam2392>` and :user:`Sérgio Pereira <sergiormpereira>` :pr:`28622`\\n407: \\n408: - |Enhancement| The verbosity of :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier`\\n409:   and :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingRegressor` got a more granular control. Now,\\n410:   `verbose = 1` prints only summary messages, `verbose >= 2` prints the full\\n411:   information as before.\\n412:   By :user:`Christian Lorentzen <lorentzenchr>` :pr:`28179`\\n413: \\n414: - |API| The parameter `algorithm` of :class:`ensemble.AdaBoostClassifier` is deprecated\\n415:   and will be removed in 1.8.\\n416:   By :user:`Jérémie du Boisberranger <jeremiedbb>` :pr:`29997`\\n417: \\n418: :mod:`sklearn.feature_extraction`\\n419: ---------------------------------\\n420: \\n421: - |Fix| :class:`feature_extraction.text.TfidfVectorizer` now correctly preserves the\\n422:   `dtype` of `idf_` based on the input data.\\n423:   By :user:`Guillaume Lemaitre <glemaitre>` :pr:`30022`\\n424: \\n425: :mod:`sklearn.frozen`\\n426: ---------------------\\n427: \\n428: - |MajorFeature| :class:`~sklearn.frozen.FrozenEstimator` is now introduced which allows\\n429:   freezing an estimator. This means calling `.fit` on it has no effect, and doing a\\n430:   `clone(frozenestimator)` returns the same estimator instead of an unfitted clone.\\n431:   :pr:`29705` By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`29705`\\n432: \\n433: :mod:`sklearn.impute`\\n434: ---------------------\\n435: \\n436: - |Fix| :class:`impute.KNNImputer` excludes samples with nan distances when\\n437:   computing the mean value for uniform weights.\\n438:   By :user:`Xuefeng Xu <xuefeng-xu>` :pr:`29135`\\n439: \\n440: - |Fix| When `min_value` and `max_value` are array-like and some features are dropped due to\\n441:   `keep_empty_features=False`, :class:`impute.IterativeImputer` no longer raises an\\n442:   error and now indexes correctly.\\n443:   By :user:`Guntitat Sawadwuthikul <gunsodo>` :pr:`29451`\\n444: \\n445: - |Fix| Fixed :class:`impute.IterativeImputer` to make sure that it does not skip\\n446:   the iterative process when `keep_empty_features` is set to `True`.\\n447:   By :user:`Arif Qodari <arifqodari>` :pr:`29779`\\n448: \\n449: - |API| Add a warning in :class:`impute.SimpleImputer` when `keep_empty_feature=False` and\\n450:   `strategy=\\\"constant\\\"`. In this case empty features are not dropped and this behaviour\\n451:   will change in 1.8.\\n452:   By :user:`Arthur Courselle <ArthurCourselle>` and :user:`Simon Riou <simon-riou>` :pr:`29950`\\n453: \\n454: :mod:`sklearn.linear_model`\\n455: ---------------------------\\n456: \\n457: - |Enhancement| The `solver=\\\"newton-cholesky\\\"` in\\n458:   :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegression` and\\n459:   :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegressionCV` is extended to support the full\\n460:   multinomial loss in a multiclass setting.\\n461:   By :user:`Christian Lorentzen <lorentzenchr>` :pr:`28840`\\n462: \\n463: - |Fix| In :class:`linear_model.Ridge` and :class:`linear_model.RidgeCV`, after `fit`,\\n464:   the `coef_` attribute is now of shape `(n_samples,)` like other linear models.\\n465:   By :user:`Maxwell Liu<MaxwellLZH>`, `Guillaume Lemaitre`_, and `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`19746`\\n466: \\n467: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegressionCV` corrects sample weight handling\\n468:   for the calculation of test scores.\\n469:   By :user:`Shruti Nath <snath-xoc>` :pr:`29419`\\n470: \\n471: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.LassoCV` and :class:`linear_model.ElasticNetCV` now\\n472:   take sample weights into accounts to define the search grid for the internally tuned\\n473:   `alpha` hyper-parameter.\\n474:   By :user:`John Hopfensperger <s-banach>` and :user:`Shruti Nath <snath-xoc>` :pr:`29442`\\n475: \\n476: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegression`, :class:`linear_model.PoissonRegressor`,\\n477:   :class:`linear_model.GammaRegressor`, :class:`linear_model.TweedieRegressor`\\n478:   now take sample weights into account to decide when to fall back to `solver='lbfgs'`\\n479:   whenever `solver='newton-cholesky'` becomes numerically unstable.\\n480:   By :user:`Antoine Baker <antoinebaker>` :pr:`29818`\\n481: \\n482: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.RidgeCV` now properly uses predictions on the same scale as\\n483:   the target seen during `fit`. These predictions are stored in `cv_results_` when\\n484:   `scoring != None`. Previously, the predictions were rescaled by the square root of the\\n485:   sample weights and offset by the mean of the target, leading to an incorrect estimate\\n486:   of the score.\\n487:   By :user:`Guillaume Lemaitre <glemaitre>`,\\n488:   :user:`Jérôme Dockes <jeromedockes>` and\\n489:   :user:`Hanmin Qin <qinhanmin2014>` :pr:`29842`\\n490: \\n491: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.RidgeCV` now properly supports custom multioutput scorers\\n492:   by letting the scorer manage the multioutput averaging. Previously, the predictions\\n493:   and true targets were both squeezed to a 1D array before computing the error.\\n494:   By :user:`Guillaume Lemaitre <glemaitre>` :pr:`29884`\\n495: \\n496: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.LinearRegression` now sets the `cond` parameter when\\n497:   calling the `scipy.linalg.lstsq` solver on dense input data. This ensures\\n498:   more numerically robust results on rank-deficient data. In particular, it\\n499:   empirically fixes the expected equivalence property between fitting with\\n500:   reweighted or with repeated data points.\\n501:   By :user:`Antoine Baker <antoinebaker>` :pr:`30040`\\n502: \\n503: - |Fix| :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegression` and and other linear models that\\n504:   accept `solver=\\\"newton-cholesky\\\"` now report the correct number of iterations\\n505:   when they fall back to the `\\\"lbfgs\\\"` solver because of a rank deficient\\n506:   Hessian matrix.\\n507:   By :user:`Olivier Grisel <ogrisel>` :pr:`30100`\\n508: \\n509: - |Fix| :class:`~sklearn.linear_model.SGDOneClassSVM` now correctly inherits from\\n510:   :class:`~sklearn.base.OutlierMixin` and the tags are correctly set.\\n511:   By :user:`Guillaume Lemaitre <glemaitre>` :pr:`30227`\\n512: \\n513: - |API| Deprecates `copy_X` in :class:`linear_model.TheilSenRegressor` as the parameter\\n514:   has no effect. `copy_X` will be removed in 1.8.\\n515:   By :user:`Adam Li <adam2392>` :pr:`29105`\\n516: \\n517: :mod:`sklearn.manifold`\\n518: -----------------------\\n519: \\n520: - |Efficiency| :func:`manifold.locally_linear_embedding` and\\n521:   :class:`manifold.LocallyLinearEmbedding` now allocate more efficiently the memory of\\n522:   sparse matrices in the Hessian, Modified and LTSA methods.\\n523:   By :user:`Giorgio Angelotti <giorgioangel>` :pr:`28096`\\n524: \\n525: :mod:`sklearn.metrics`\\n526: ----------------------\\n527: \\n528: - |Fix| The functions :func:`metrics.mean_squared_log_error` and\\n549:   :func:`metrics.root_mean_squared_log_error` now check whether the inputs are within\\n550:   the correct domain for the function :math:`y=\\\\log(1+x)`, rather than\\n551:   :math:`y=\\\\log(x)`. The functions :func:`metrics.mean_absolute_error`,\\n552:   :func:`metrics.mean_absolute_percentage_error`, :func:`metrics.mean_squared_error`\\n553:   and :func:`metrics.root_mean_squared_error` now explicitly check whether a scalar\\n554:   will be returned when `multioutput=uniform_average`.\\n555:   By :user:`Virgil Chan <virchan>` :pr:`29709`\\n556: \\n557: - |API| The `assert_all_finite` parameter of functions\\n558:   :func:`metrics.pairwise.check_pairwise_arrays` and :func:`metrics.pairwise_distances`\\n559:   is renamed into `ensure_all_finite`. `force_all_finite` will be removed in 1.8.\\n560:   By :user:`Jérémie du Boisberranger <jeremiedb>` :pr:`29404`\\n561: \\n562: - |API| `scoring=\\\"neg_max_error\\\"` should be used instead of `scoring=\\\"max_error\\\"`\\n563:   which is now deprecated.\\n564:   By :user:`Farid \\\"Freddie\\\" Taba <artificialfintelligence>` :pr:`29462`\\n565: \\n566: - |API| The default value of the `response_method` parameter of\\n567:   :func:`metrics.make_scorer` will change from `None` to `\\\"predict\\\"` and `None` will be\\n568:   removed in 1.8. In the mean time, `None` is equivalent to `\\\"predict\\\"`.\\n569:   By :user:`Jérémie du Boisberranger <jeremiedb>` :pr:`30001`\\n570: \\n571: :mod:`sklearn.model_selection`\\n572: ------------------------------\\n573: \\n574: - |Enhancement| :class:`neighbors.NearestNeighbors`,\\n591:   :class:`neighbors.KNeighborsClassifier`,\\n592:   :class:`neighbors.KNeighborsRegressor`,\\n593:   :class:`neighbors.RadiusNeighborsClassifier`,\\n594:   :class:`neighbors.RadiusNeighborsRegressor`,\\n595:   :class:`neighbors.KNeighborsTransformer`,\\n596:   :class:`neighbors.RadiusNeighborsTransformer`, and\\n597:   :class:`neighbors.LocalOutlierFactor`\\n598:   now work with `metric=\\\"nan_euclidean\\\"`, supporting `nan` inputs.\\n599:   By :user:`Carlo Lemos <vitaliset>`, `Guillaume Lemaitre`_, and `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`25330`\\n600: \\n601: - |Enhancement| Add :meth:`neighbors.NearestCentroid.decision_function`,\\n602:   :meth:`neighbors.NearestCentroid.predict_proba` and\\n603:   :meth:`neighbors.NearestCentroid.predict_log_proba`\\n604:   to the :class:`neighbors.NearestCentroid` estimator class.\\n605:   Support the case when `X` is sparse and `shrinking_threshold`\\n606:   is not `None` in :class:`neighbors.NearestCentroid`.\\n607:   By :user:`Matthew Ning <NoPenguinsLand>` :pr:`26689`\\n608: \\n609: - |Enhancement| Make `predict`, `predict_proba`, and `score` of\\n610:   :class:`neighbors.KNeighborsClassifier` and\\n611:   :class:`neighbors.RadiusNeighborsClassifier` accept `X=None` as input. In this case\\n612:   predictions for all training set points are returned, and points are not included\\n613:   into their own neighbors.\\n614:   By :user:`Dmitry Kobak <dkobak>` :pr:`30047`\\n615: \\n616: - |Fix| :class:`neighbors.LocalOutlierFactor` raises a warning in the `fit` method\\n617:   when duplicate values in the training data lead to inaccurate outlier detection.\\n618:   By :user:`Henrique Caroço <HenriqueProj>` :pr:`28773`\\n619: \\n620: :mod:`sklearn.neural_network`\\n621: -----------------------------\\n622: \\n623: - |Fix| :class:`neural_network.MLPRegressor` does no longer crash when the model\\n624:   diverges and that `early_stopping` is enabled.\\n625:   By :user:`Marc Bresson <MarcBresson>` :pr:`29773`\\n626: \\n627: :mod:`sklearn.pipeline`\\n628: -----------------------\\n629: \\n630: - |MajorFeature| :class:`pipeline.Pipeline` can now transform metadata up to the step requiring the\\n631:   metadata, which can be set using the `transform_input` parameter.\\n632:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`28901`\\n633: \\n634: - |Enhancement| :class:`pipeline.Pipeline` now warns about not being fitted before calling methods\\n635:   that require the pipeline to be fitted. This warning will become an error in 1.8.\\n636:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`29868`\\n637: \\n638: - |Fix| Fixed an issue with tags and estimator type of :class:`~sklearn.pipeline.Pipeline`\\n639:   when pipeline is empty. This allows the HTML representation of an empty\\n640:   pipeline to be rendered correctly.\\n641:   By :user:`Gennaro Daniele Acciaro <gdacciaro>` :pr:`30203`\\n642: \\n643: :mod:`sklearn.preprocessing`\\n644: ----------------------------\\n645: \\n646: - |Enhancement| Added `warn` option to `handle_unknown` parameter in\\n647:   :class:`preprocessing.OneHotEncoder`.\\n648:   By :user:`Gleb Levitski <glevv>` :pr:`28637`\\n649: \\n650: - |Enhancement| The HTML representation of :class:`preprocessing.FunctionTransformer`\\n651:   will show the function name in the label.\\n652:   By :user:`Yao Xiao <Charlie-XIAO>` :pr:`29158`\\n653: \\n654: - |Fix| :class:`preprocessing.PowerTransformer` now uses `scipy.special.inv_boxcox`\\n655:   to output `nan` if the input of BoxCox's inverse is invalid.\\n656:   By :user:`Xuefeng Xu <xuefeng-xu>` :pr:`27875`\\n657: \\n658: :mod:`sklearn.semi_supervised`\\n659: ------------------------------\\n660: \\n661: - |API| :class:`semi_supervised.SelfTrainingClassifier`\\n662:   deprecated the `base_estimator` parameter in favor of `estimator`.\\n663:   By :user:`Adam Li <adam2392>` :pr:`28494`\\n664: \\n665: :mod:`sklearn.tree`\\n666: -------------------\\n667: \\n668: - |Feature| :class:`tree.ExtraTreeClassifier` and :class:`tree.ExtraTreeRegressor` now\\n669:   support missing-values in the data matrix ``X``. Missing-values are handled by\\n670:   randomly moving all of the samples to the left, or right child node as the tree is\\n671:   traversed.\\n672:   By :user:`Adam Li <adam2392>` and :user:`Loïc Estève <lesteve>` :pr:`27966`, :pr:`30318`\\n673: \\n674: - |Fix| Escape double quotes for labels and feature names when exporting trees to Graphviz\\n675:   format.\\n676:   By :user:`Santiago M. Mola <smola>`. :pr:`17575`\\n677: \\n678: :mod:`sklearn.utils`\\n679: --------------------\\n680: \\n681: - |Enhancement| :func:`utils.check_array` now accepts `ensure_non_negative`\\n682:   to check for negative values in the passed array, until now only available through\\n683:   calling :func:`utils.check_non_negative`.\\n684:   By :user:`Tamara Atanasoska <tamaraatanasoska>` :pr:`29540`\\n685: \\n686: - |Enhancement| :func:`~sklearn.utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` and\\n687:   :func:`~sklearn.utils.estimator_checks.parametrize_with_checks` now check and fail if\\n688:   the classifier has the `tags.classifier_tags.multi_class = False` tag but does not\\n689:   fail on multi-class data.\\n690:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`29874`\\n691: \\n692: - |Enhancement| :func:`utils.validation.check_is_fitted` now passes on stateless\\n693:   estimators. An estimator can indicate it's stateless by setting the `requires_fit`\\n694:   tag. See :ref:`estimator_tags` for more information.\\n695:   By :user:`Adrin Jalali <adrinjalali>` :pr:`29880`\\n696: \\n697: - |Enhancement| Changes to :func:`~utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` and\\n698:   :func:`~utils.estimator_checks.parametrize_with_checks`.\\n699: \\n700:   - :func:`~utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` introduces new arguments:\\n701:     ``on_skip``, ``on_fail``, and ``callback`` to control the behavior of the check\\n702:     runner. Refer to the API documentation for more details.\\n703: \\n704:   - ``generate_only=True`` is deprecated in\\n705:     :func:`~utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator`. Use\\n706:     :func:`~utils.estimator_checks.estimator_checks_generator` instead.\\n707: \\n708:   - The ``_xfail_checks`` estimator tag is now removed, and now in order to indicate\\n709:     which tests are expected to fail, you can pass a dictionary to the\\n710:     :func:`~utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` as the ``expected_failed_checks``\\n711:     parameter. Similarly, the ``expected_failed_checks`` parameter in\\n712:     :func:`~utils.estimator_checks.parametrize_with_checks` can be used, which is a\\n713:     callable returning a dictionary of the form::\\n714: \\n715:         {\\n716:             \\\"check_name\\\": \\\"reason to mark this check as xfail\\\",\\n717:         }\\n718: \\n719:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`30149`\\n720: \\n721: - |Fix| :func:`utils.estimator_checks.parametrize_with_checks` and\\n722:   :func:`utils.estimator_checks.check_estimator` now support estimators that\\n723:   have `set_output` called on them.\\n724:   By :user:`Adrin Jalali <adrinjalali>` :pr:`29869`\\n725: \\n726: - |API| The `assert_all_finite` parameter of functions :func:`utils.check_array`,\\n727:   :func:`utils.check_X_y`, :func:`utils.as_float_array` is renamed into\\n728:   `ensure_all_finite`. `force_all_finite` will be removed in 1.8.\\n729:   By :user:`Jérémie du Boisberranger <jeremiedb>` :pr:`29404`\\n730: \\n731: - |API| `utils.estimator_checks.check_sample_weights_invariance`\\n732:   replaced by\\n733:   `utils.estimator_checks.check_sample_weight_equivalence_on_dense_data`\\n734:   which uses integer (including zero) weights and\\n735:   `utils.estimator_checks.check_sample_weight_equivalence_on_sparse_data`\\n736:   which does the same on sparse data.\\n737:   By :user:`Antoine Baker <antoinebaker>` :pr:`29818`, :pr:`30137`\\n738: \\n739: - |API| Using `_estimator_type` to set the estimator type is deprecated. Inherit from\\n740:   :class:`~sklearn.base.ClassifierMixin`, :class:`~sklearn.base.RegressorMixin`,\\n741:   :class:`~sklearn.base.TransformerMixin`, or :class:`~sklearn.base.OutlierMixin`\\n742:   instead. Alternatively, you can set `estimator_type` in :class:`~sklearn.utils.Tags`\\n743:   in the `__sklearn_tags__` method.\\n744:   By `Adrin Jalali`_ :pr:`30122`\\n745: \\n746: .. rubric:: Code and documentation contributors\\n747: \\n748: Thanks to everyone who has contributed to the maintenance and improvement of\\n749: the project since version 1.5, including:\\n750: \\n751: Aaron Schumacher, Abdulaziz Aloqeely, abhi-jha, Acciaro Gennaro Daniele, Adam \\n752: J.
